title: silene foetida subsp. gayana
intro: Silene foetida subsp. gayana, also known as the 'stinking catchfly,' is a plant species in the carnation family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and antifungal properties, which can be used to treat wounds, infections, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Silene foetida subsp. gayana include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making it a useful remedy for cuts, burns, and other minor injuries. The plant can be prepared as a salve or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote wound healing and prevent infection.
